short_description: Download & Save Porndroids Videos Easily for Offline Viewing (Browser Extension)
long_description: This extension is built around Porndroids' distinctive droids/tech-ish brand identity and its clean slug-only /video/{slug}/ page structure. It accounts for the way Porndroids delegates playback through an embedded iframe rather than exposing the media URL directly on the outer page, making stream discovery more straightforward.

faqs.question: What Porndroids pages does this extension work with?
faqs.answer: It works with video pages that use the /video/{slug}/ URL pattern, such as /video/broke-amateurs/.

faqs.question: Why does Porndroids use an iframe for playback?
faqs.answer: Porndroids delegates video playback to an embedded iframe rather than exposing the media URL directly on the outer page. The extension accounts for this behavior.

faqs.question: What media formats are supported?
faqs.answer: The extension targets M3U8 and MP4 sources as confirmed from the page structure.

faqs.question: Is this extension affiliated with Porndroids?
faqs.answer: No. This is an independent tool built by SERP Apps for user convenience.

faqs.question: Do I need an account on Porndroids to use this extension?
faqs.answer: No account is required. You can use the extension on publicly accessible video pages.

why: Porndroids uses a clean page structure where each video lives at a simple /video/{slug}/ URL. However, the actual media playback happens inside an embedded iframe rather than directly on the page. This separation means the video source is not immediately visible, and standard browser tools may not easily capture the stream.

Porndroids Downloader bridges that gap by working with the iframe layer to locate the media source. It is built specifically for Porndroids' page model and brand identity, so you get a tool that understands how the site actually delivers its content rather than a generic downloader that may miss the embedded player.
how_it_works: Install the extension from the latest release.
Open Porndroids and go to a supported video page.
Start playback so the extension can detect the media.
Open the popup or use the on-page controls.
Choose the quality option you want.
Start the download and wait for the MP4 export to finish.
tutorial: Install the Porndroids Downloader extension from the latest GitHub release.
Open your browser and navigate to Porndroids.
Find a video page that follows the /video/{slug}/ URL pattern, such as /video/broke-amateurs/.
Click on the video to start playback on the page.
Click the Porndroids Downloader icon in your browser toolbar to open the popup.
The extension will detect the media source from the embedded player.
Select your preferred quality option if multiple are available.
Click the download button and save the resulting MP4 file to your device.

troubleshooting: The extension does not detect any media on a Porndroids page Make sure the video is playing or has been loaded on the page. The extension needs the iframe to load its content before it can detect the media source.
The download starts but fails partway through Check your internet connection and try again. If the issue persists, the video source may be temporarily unavailable on Porndroids.
I see an error about unsupported page format Ensure you are on a Porndroids page that follows the /video/{slug}/ pattern. Other page types may not be supported.
The extension icon appears grayed out The extension only activates on supported Porndroids video pages. Navigate to a valid video page and refresh if needed.
The downloaded file will not play Make sure you have a media player that supports MP4 files. Most modern players including VLC, Windows Media Player, and QuickTime should work.
